mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor market conditions, indicated by rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE), signal robust corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through demand-side confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods where PAYEMS > 148,000 and UNRATE < 4.2% are negative in > 40% of cases, or pairwise correlation between PAYEMS/UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust economic demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods where GDPC1 > 19,400 and HOUST > 1,500 are negative in > 35% of cases, or the joint predictive power of GDPC1/HOUST drops below 0.25 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 280 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods where CPIAUCSL > 280 are positive in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong supply-side capacity utilization, which signals corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods where INDPRO > 101.5 and RSAFS > 639,000 are negative in > 30% of cases, or the interaction term between INDPRO/RSAFS loses significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods where FEDFUNDS > 0.1% and CPIAUCSL > 278 are positive in > 45% of cases, or the joint effect of FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects tight labor markets and rising wage pressures, which boost consumer spending and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods of UNRATE < 4.2% are negative in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods where INDPRO > 101.5 are negative in > 40% of cases, or the predictive power of INDPRO on SPX returns drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.
